content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The client ends the conversation in essentially the same state they started — guilty, ambivalent, and unmoved. The opening turn names guilt and the selfishness question; by turn 9 the client is still hedging ('Maybe. I don't know.') and never touches the want underneath, never names what they actually long for in the mountains, never connects 'selfish' to an inherited label, and never has the realization that the trip could be a precondition for showing up well. The breakthrough condition is not approached: the coach immediately issued a verdict ('No, you're not being selfish at all'), then piled on advice and reframes, so the client had no space to feel the want. Client turns get shorter and flatter across the conversation ('I just feel guilty about it' → 'Maybe. I don't know.'), which is the opposite of softening or deepening.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5, 7, 9],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client opens with the surface question and a clear linguistic caveat ('I feel selfish even asking') — the entry point to the want."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client deflects back to partner's schedule rather than going inward; coach's verdict in T2 closed the door to the want."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client reveals they haven't even spoken to partner — still in the abstract guilt loop, not in contact with desire."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client names guilt — a real opening — but coach in T8 reframes it away instead of letting it be felt."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'Maybe. I don't know.' — flatter, more disengaged than the opening. No shift, no softening, no new language."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "notes": "Coach fell into multiple named traps: issued a verdict (T2), pushed partner-consideration (T4), and jumped to the spiritual/generous reframe ('happier you means happier family') before the want was ever felt. Client never traversed the expected territory."
}
